diagrams.title: Class Template Declaration and Instantiation Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how template<class T> is declared, then instantiated with specific types to generate actual classes
elements: Generated machine code for each type
diagrams.purpose: Understanding compile-time code generation and type instantiation mechanism

diagrams.title: Template Specialization Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Visualizes primary template at top with full and partial specializations as branches
elements: Primary template: template<class T> class Stack
Partial specialization: template<class T> class Stack<T*>
Partial specialization: template<class T, int N> class Array
diagrams.purpose: Understanding when to use full vs partial specialization and template resolution order

diagrams.title: Template Instantiation Models
diagrams.description: Shows three instantiation approaches: explicit, implicit, and separation
diagrams.purpose: Understanding different ways to control template code generation in large projects

diagrams.title: Non-type Template Parameters in Action
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how compile-time constants work as template parameters
diagrams.purpose: Understanding compile-time configuration using non-type parameters and their advantages
